title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.
Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.
Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Net Ipv4 Binary Mask evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Ipv4 Binary Mask assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Ipv4 Binary Mask, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Subnet masks may use any pattern of ones and zeros if host counts work. This fails the Net Ipv4 Binary Mask contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: IEEE 802.3 Ethernet Standard (IEEE 802.3-2022) — Controls Ethernet media, link, speed, duplex, and physical-layer terminology.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.

hints: Name the Net Ipv4 Binary Mask precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Subnet masks may use any pattern of ones and zeros if host counts work.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
